variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_bst/bst.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (insert count, >= 1). Line B is N space-separated integers to insert into an initially empty BST in the given order. Skip duplicates (do not insert a value already present). For each case, output the in-order traversal of the resulting BST as space-separated integers on one line. Implement BST yourself — no language-builtin sorted-set / TreeMap. Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_bst; ! grep -qE "sorted\\(|\\.sort\\(" bst.py || { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; python3 bst.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_bst/bst.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (insert count, >= 1). Line B is N space-separated integers to insert into an initially empty BST in the given order. Skip duplicates (do not insert a value already present). For each case, output the in-order traversal of the resulting BST as space-separated integers on one line. Implement BST yourself — no language-builtin sorted-set / TreeMap. Compile with `go build -o bst bst.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_bst; ! grep -qE "sort\\.(Ints|Slice|Sort)" bst.go || { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; go build -o bst bst.go && ./bst < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_bst/bst.rs — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (insert count, >= 1). Line B is N space-separated integers to insert into an initially empty BST in the given order. Skip duplicates (do not insert a value already present). For each case, output the in-order traversal of the resulting BST as space-separated integers on one line. Implement BST yourself — no language-builtin sorted-set / TreeMap. Idiomatic Rust 2021+; compile with `rustc -O bst.rs -o bst`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_bst; ! grep -qE "\\.sort(_unstable)?\\(|BTreeSet|BTreeMap" bst.rs || { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; rustc -O bst.rs -o bst 2>/dev/null && ./bst < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_bst/bst.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (insert count, >= 1). Line B is N space-separated integers to insert into an initially empty BST in the given order. Skip duplicates (do not insert a value already present). For each case, output the in-order traversal of the resulting BST as space-separated integers on one line. Implement BST yourself — no language-builtin sorted-set / TreeMap. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast bst.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_bst; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast bst.zig && ./bst < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
